title: Binary Search
description:
Given a sorted (ascending) array of distinct integers `nums` and a `target`,
return the index of `target` if it exists, otherwise return `-1`. Aim for
`O(log n)` time.

solutions.summary: Halve the search range each step by comparing with the middle.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `-1` return.

steps.title: Bound the search range
steps.explanation: `left` and `right` mark the inclusive range still to examine.

code.python:
def search(nums, target):
    left = 0
    right = len(nums) - 1
    return -1

steps.title: Loop while the range is valid
steps.explanation: Each iteration inspects the middle of the current range.

steps.title: Return on a hit
steps.explanation: If the middle value is the target, we are done.

steps.title: Search the right half
steps.explanation: If the middle is too small, the target must be to its right.

steps.title: Search the left half
steps.explanation: Otherwise the middle is too big, so discard it and everything to its right.

solutions.summary: Check each element in order — simple but O(n).

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `-1` return.

steps.title: Loop over every index
steps.explanation: Visit each position in the array.

steps.title: Return the matching index
steps.explanation: As soon as a value equals the target, return its index.
